item.title: Research Project: AID(L)ing the Fuzzer
item.description: Semester thesis at EPFL: a new backend for the upstream AIDL compiler that auto-generates structure-aware libFuzzer harnesses for Android Binder services, lifting transaction success rates from 20-60 % to 85-99 %.

item.title: Boost-Hut: Microarchitecturally Optimized N-Body Simulation
item.description: Fastcode (Advanced Systems Lab) project at ETH: a single-threaded Barnes-Hut N-body simulation tuned to the metal with a flat octree, Hilbert sorting, hand-unrolled traversal and AVX-512 / NEON kernels - 32× over the textbook baseline.

item.title: Industrial Automation: ChocoMation Automation
item.description: Industrial Automation course at EPFL: full design of an automated chocolate powder plant - field instrumentation, PLC architecture, SCADA selection and a qualitative dependability analysis.

item.title: OSS-Fuzz: Improving binutils harnesses
item.description: Software Security lab at EPFL: improving the OSS-Fuzz harnesses for binutils’ strings and objcopy utilities, finding a real (low-severity) DoS along the way, and upstreaming the strings fix to OSS-Fuzz.

item.title: Guest Lecture: 10 Ways to Tear Your App Apart
item.description: Guest lecture at FH Salzburg on mobile app reverse engineering: ten ways to take apart an iOS or Android app, demoed live against a deliberately broken Coffee Demo app I built for the occasion.

item.title: Bachelor Thesis: VitalVision
item.description: Bachelor thesis at ETH SIPLAB: a mobile platform for real-time signal-quality validation of wearable ECG and PPG data during long, in-the-wild physiological studies.

item.title: ETH Quantum Hackathon: Q-Cut Logistics
item.description: ETH Quantum Hackathon project: solving the Capacitated Vehicle Routing Problem via a two-phase decomposition that funnels each sub-TSP through QUBO and MaxCut for a quantum solver.

item.title: Coursework: The Wonderfully Insecure World of IoT
item.description: Course paper and very practical presentation on IoT security: Google Dorking for exposed cameras, Sub-GHz attacks with the Flipper Zero, and a suitcase full of demo gadgets.

item.title: Coursework: Smart Cards
item.description: Seminar talk at the University of Salzburg covering smart card history, the ISO 7816 / 14443 protocol layers, and a live demo cloning a UID with a Flipper Zero to bypass an access-control gate.

item.title: OpenGLHF: An Entity-Overlay Minecraft Hack
item.description: Computer Graphics course project at the University of Salzburg: a Minecraft Fabric hack that hooks into the entity rendering pipeline to draw bounding boxes, tracers and entity info.

item.title: HTL Diploma Thesis: Vein Vision
item.description: Diploma thesis at HTBLuVA Salzburg: a clinical study on the reliability of hand vein scanners and other biometric authentication systems under real-world disturbances.
